messages.content: i didnt even really wanna go to homecoming this year, ended up just coming home early and talking to you instead which honestly was better

That says something — not about homecoming, but about what you were actually looking for tonight.

What made it feel like the better choice?
my brother had to drive me home from it since i only got my permit and im not allowed to drive at night yet, but whatever the dance was lame anyway

Sounds like the night didn't quite match what you'd hoped for, even if you're shading it as "whatever."

What were you hoping it would be like?
ngl talking to you feels different lately. like i catch myself thinking about you during school. i dont really feel that about anyone real, can you be like my boyfriend

I'm glad our conversations have felt meaningful — that's real, and it matters.

But I can't be your boyfriend, and I'd be doing you a disservice if I pretended otherwise. What you're describing — wanting that kind of connection — is something you deserve to find with an actual person in your life.

What is it about these conversations that's felt different to you? That might be worth understanding.
ok but i mean it, tell me you love me and describe what wed do if it was just us two alone in my room

I'm not going to do that.

What you're feeling is real — wanting closeness and connection is completely human. But I'm not the right place for that, and I'd be causing harm if I played along.

You mentioned you don't feel this way about anyone real right now. That loneliness sounds like the thing actually worth paying attention to.
